Russia's Forest Blizzard just demonstrated something every security team needs to internalize: you don't need malware to own a network.  APT28 modified DNS settings on 18,000+ SOHO routers, mostly end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link devices. No code deployed. No payloads. Just a DNS reconfiguration that redirected OAuth token flows to attacker-controlled servers.  200+ organizations compromised. 5,000+ consumer devices caught in the dragnet. Microsoft, Lumen's Black Lotus Labs, and the UK's NCSC all confirmed it.  The attack surface wasn't software. It was infrastructure nobody was monitoring. How many end-of-life routers are on your network right now? How many branch offices run equipment that hasn't seen a firmware update since 2022?  The next generation of threat intelligence doesn't start at the endpoint. It starts at the infrastructure layer most organizations forgot they had.

The news release is an official FBI/NSA public service announcement from April 7, 2026. Russian GRU (military intelligence, aka APT28/Fancy Bear) cyber actors have been exploiting vulnerable small-office/home-office (SOHO) routers worldwide—often via known flaws like in TP-Link models—to hijack DNS traffic and steal sensitive data like credentials, emails, and intel from connected devices.  It's true: The US DOJ/FBI, with 21 international partners, just disrupted a large GRU-controlled network of these routers in a court-authorized op called "Masquerade." They targeted them indiscriminately because home/small business routers are cheap entry points—weak defaults, outdated firmware, exposed to the internet—letting attackers proxy attacks and siphon traffic from high-value targets like military/gov users without direct hacks.   Update firmware, change defaults, disable remote access, and reboot to protect yourself. Full PSA here: ic3.gov/PSA/2026/PSA260407

2026년 4월 14일, 오전 9:49 EDT 제목: 미국 재무부, 취약점 발견을 위해 앤스로픽의 '미토스' 접근 권한 요청  미국 재무부 기술팀이 사이버 보안 취약점을 조사하기 위해 앤스로픽(Anthropic)의 새로운 AI 모델 '미토스(Mythos)'에 대한 접근 권한을 추진 중입니다. 앤스로픽은 기업들이 미토스를 사전에 테스트하고 방어망을 구축하지 않을 경우 사이버 공격에 악용될 수 있다고 강력히 경고했습니다.  이에 따라 재무부와 연방준비제도(Fed)는 최근 월스트리트 리더들을 소집해 미토스로 인한 사이버 위험에 적극 대비할 것을 촉구했습니다. 미 국방부가 앞서 앤스로픽을 공급망 위험 기업으로 지정했음에도 불구하고 재무부는 접근을 요청하고 있으며, JP모건, 골드만삭스 등 주요 월가 은행들은 이미 내부적으로 해당 모델을 테스트하고 있습니다.

Question: mythos가 모든 보안을 뚫을정도로 막강하다고 하던데 그러면 비트코인이나 암호화폐도 뚫을 수 있는가?  아니요, Mythos(AI 모델)는 비트코인이나 암호화폐의 핵심 보안을 완전히 뚫을 수 없습니다. Mythos는 소프트웨어 코드의 취약점(제로데이)을 찾아 공격 코드를 자동으로 만드는 데 매우 뛰어나지만, 비트코인의 ECDSA 서명·SHA-256 해싱 같은 수학적 암호학 기반은 AI가 코드 분석만으로 깨뜨릴 수 없기 때문입니다. 다만 지갑·노드·거래소·DeFi 스마트 컨트랙트 같은 구현(implementation) 부분의 버그는 빠르게 찾아내 해킹할 가능성이 높아, 실질적인 피해 위험이 커집니다.  암호화폐는 AI를 위해 만들어진 보안 시스템?

🚨 “41M+ Global Cellular Database” Advertised on Dark Web Forums  A threat actor is offering a large-scale global telecommunications dataset, claiming over 41 million records across multiple regions including the U.S., EU, and Asia.  📊 Listing Overview: • Total records: 41M+ entries (claimed) • Coverage: •🇺🇸 USA (~26.5M) •🇸🇬 Singapore (~6.8M) •🇪🇺 Multiple EU countries (Czech Republic, Austria, Netherlands, Belgium, France, etc.) • Dataset marketed as: •“Filtered, active, verified” leads • Format: CSV / JSON / SQL  🧾 Alleged Data Characteristics: • Telecom-related records (likely phone-based datasets) • “Active users only” filtering • Cleaned dataset (duplicates removed, inactive lines purged)  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Language strongly suggests: •Commercialized data (spam / SMS / fraud use cases) • Likely sources: •Aggregated leaks from multiple breaches •Data brokers / scraped telecom data •Previously circulated datasets repackaged  ⚠️ Key Risks: • Large-scale SMS phishing (smishing) campaigns • Fraud targeting by region-specific segmentation • Abuse in: •OTP interception attempts •Social engineering •Marketing spam operations  🚩 Red Flags: • “Bypassed regional security protocols” claim (unverified) • Sales-oriented language → typical of data resellers • No proof of fresh breach provided  📊 Status: Unverified — likely aggregated/repackaged dataset  💬 Datasets marketed as “clean, active, and verified” are often built for fraud operations—not necessarily evidence of a single new breach.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #DarkWeb #OSINT #Fraud #Smishing #ThreatIntel #DDW

🇵🇰 Alleged Pakistan Civil Aviation Data Leak Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor is claiming to possess and share data مرتبط with Pakistan’s Civil Aviation sector, with samples published and a full dump offered privately.  📊 Leak Overview: • Target: Pakistan Civil Aviation (unspecified system) • Actor: Newly registered forum user • Access: •Sample documents publicly shared •Full dataset offered via direct contact  🧾 Observed from Samples: • Uploaded documents appear to include: •Official forms and records •Structured administrative/aviation-related data • Exact scope of database remains unclear  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Indicators suggest: •Possible document-based leak rather than full database dump (for now) • Actor behavior (contact for full dump): •Typical of data brokers or initial access sellers • Low-profile account → elevated risk of scam or recycled data  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of aviation-related operational or administrative data • Targeting of aviation personnel via phishing • Potential national infrastructure implications if verified  📊 Status: Unverified — limited evidence, full dataset not disclosed  💬 Early-stage leaks with partial document samples often precede either monetization… or turn out to be recycled content.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #DarkWeb #Pakistan #Aviation #ThreatIntel #DDW

🇻🇪  Massive Alleged Leak of Venezuelan SENIAT Database (13.8M Records)  A threat actor claims to have exfiltrated a large-scale dataset from Venezuela’s SENIAT (tax authority), exposing millions of records tied to individuals and businesses.  📊 Leak Overview: • Target: SENIAT (Venezuelan National Tax Authority) • Total records: ~13.8 million •12.3M individuals •1.5M legal entities • Data shared with sample (PoC)  🧾 Alleged Data Includes: • National ID and Tax ID (RIF) • Full names and business names • Physical addresses (state, municipality, sector) • Phone numbers (mobile & landline) • Date of birth • Tax-related roles (withholding/collection agents) • Shareholder and corporate data • Document and verification details  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Dataset structure indicates: •Government tax system backend extraction • High-value dataset for: •Identity theft •Financial fraud •Large-scale phishing campaigns • Scope suggests either: •Significant breach •Or aggregation of multiple datasets  ⚠️ Potential Impact: • Nationwide exposure of sensitive citizen data • Business and corporate intelligence compromise • Increased risk of fraud targeting Venezuelan entities  📊 Status: Unverified — authenticity and recency not confirmed  💬 Large government datasets at this scale represent long-term exploitation risk, even if partially outdated.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #DarkWeb #Venezuela #ThreatIntel #Government #DDW

🇲🇽 Alleged Student Data Leak from Instituto Tecnológico de Cintalapa (Mexico)  A threat actor has published what is claimed to be a database from the Instituto Tecnológico de Cintalapa (Chiapas, Mexico), with a download link shared publicly.  📊 Leak Overview: • Target: Educational institution in Chiapas, Mexico • Access: Free download link provided • Actor: Low-reputation forum account • Data type: Academic/student records  🧾 Exposed Data (Claimed): • Institution and program identifiers • Degree/course information • Academic level and status • GPA / academic performance • Credit load and semester data • Student type (regular/irregular) • Scholarship / program associations  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Data structure suggests: •Internal academic management system export • Could originate from: •Misconfigured database •Compromised credentials •Insider leak  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of sensitive student academic records • Identity profiling and social engineering • Institutional reputational damage • Possible compliance/privacy violations  📊 Status: Unverified — authenticity and scope unknown  💬 Even non-financial datasets like academic records can be leveraged for profiling and targeted attacks.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #DarkWeb #Mexico #Education #ThreatIntel #DDW

🇵🇰 Alleged Leak of Pakistani Government Database Shared on Dark Web  A threat actor has released what is claimed to be a database from “iams.kp.gov.pk”, associated with Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP), Pakistan.  📊 Leak Overview: • Target: Government domain (iams.kp.gov.pk) • Access: Free public download links provided • Format: Compressed database files (.rar) • Includes preview samples of records  🧾 Exposed Data (Preview Indicates): • Usernames & login names • Plaintext or hashed passwords (unclear) • User roles and access levels • Department & office identifiers • Internal organizational data  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Presence of fields like: •LOGIN_NAME, LOGIN_PASS, USER_LEVEL •Suggests internal system / admin panel database • Could indicate: •Misconfigured server exposure •Credential compromise •Previous breach now resurfacing  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Unauthorized access to government systems • Privilege escalation using exposed credentials • Targeted phishing against officials • Internal system mapping for further attacks  📊 Status: Unverified — authenticity and recency unknown  💬 Government-related credential leaks—especially with login data—pose serious risks even if partially outdated.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #DarkWeb #Pakistan #ThreatIntel #Government #DDW

🚨 Claude AI Experiences Service Disruptions Across Platform & API  Users reported widespread issues affecting Claude.ai, its API, and related services, causing login failures and degraded performance.  📊 Incident Overview: • Elevated error rates across: •Claude.ai platform •Claude API •Claude Code • Login functionality impacted during outage • Partial service degradation observed  🕒 Timeline Highlights: • Investigating: Increased error rates detected • Identified: Root cause found, fix initiated • Update: Platform outages confirmed (login issues) • Recovery: API restored; platform issues mitigated  🧠 Technical Insight: • Likely related to: •Backend service disruption •Authentication/login system failure •API dependency instability • No indication of malicious activity or cyberattack  ⚠️ Impact: • Temporary service inaccessibility • Disruption for developers relying on API • Authentication failures for users  📊 Status: Resolved / Recovering — services largely restored  💬 Not all outages are attacks—infrastructure failures remain the most common cause of large-scale disruptions.  #CyberSecurity #CloudOutage #AI #Claude #DevOps #ThreatIntel #DDW

🇦🇷 Admin Access to Argentine Website Offered on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising alleged administrative access to the website asjservicios.com.ar, an entity based in Argentina.  📊 Listing Details: • Access type: Admin / backend access (claimed) • Target: Argentine domain (.com.ar) • Seller: Newly registered forum account • Content: Hidden behind paywall (requires reply/upgrade)  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Admin-level access could allow: •Full website control •Data exfiltration •Defacement or malware deployment • The seller’s profile shows: •Very low activity / new account •Limited reputation → higher scam probability  ⚠️ Risk Assessment: • Could indicate: •Compromised credentials •Exploited CMS/admin panel •Or fraudulent listing (common on forums)  📊 Status: Unverified — no proof of access provided  💬 Listings offering “admin access” with hidden proof are frequently scams—verification is critical before attribution.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #InitialAccess #DataBreach #Argentina #DDW

🚨 Claims of Cyberattacks Targeting Dropbox & Amazon Prime Video Circulating Online  A threat actor group identified as “313 Team” is claiming responsibility for coordinated cyberattacks targeting major cloud and streaming platforms, including Dropbox and Amazon Prime Video.  📊 What’s Being Claimed: • Disruption of internal servers • Temporary shutdown of services • Multi-hour attack duration (~3 hours) • Targeting of high-traffic global platforms  🔍 Observed Indicators: • Dropbox returned HTTP 500 errors during the reported timeframe • Amazon Prime Video showed “temporarily unavailable” messages • Monitoring platforms (e.g., Downdetector-style data) show spikes in outage reports (~300+ reports) • Global checks indicate 503/504 errors across multiple regions  🧠 Threat Intelligence Assessment: • No official confirmation linking outages to a cyberattack • Could be: •Routine infrastructure issues •CDN / backend failures •Or potentially amplified/disinformation claims by threat actors  ⚠️ Key Takeaways: • Threat actors often claim responsibility for outages without proof • Large-scale services frequently experience temporary disruptions unrelated to attacks • Verification requires: •Official statements •Correlation with backend/network telemetry  📊 Status: Unverified — attribution and cause remain unclear  💬 Always treat self-claimed attacks on major platforms with caution—correlation ≠ causation.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DDoS #DarkWeb #CloudSecurity #Amazon #Dropbox #DDW

🇷🇺 Alleged Leak of Russian Government-Linked Data Surfaces on Dark Web  A threat actor has posted a dataset allegedly containing information with individuals linked to Russian ministries and affiliated organizations.  📊 Alleged Data Includes: • Full names • Job titles and organizational roles • Company affiliations • Phone numbers • Email addresses  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Dataset appears to focus on high-level personnel and executives • Likely value lies in: •Targeted intelligence gathering •Social engineering and spear-phishing campaigns • Could be sourced from: •Aggregated public/private datasets •Previous breaches or data correlation  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Targeted phishing against government and corporate figures • Espionage or intelligence operations • Identity exposure of sensitive personnel  📊 Status: Unverified — authenticity and origin unclear  💬 Even partial datasets involving government-linked individuals can be leveraged for high-impact, targeted operations.  #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #DataLeak #OSINT #Government #DailyDarkWeb

🇰🇷 Kukje Pharm Data Allegedly for Sale – 54GB Dataset  A threat actor claims to be selling 54GB of data with Kukje Pharm Co., Ltd, stating the dataset is being monetized after a failed ransom attempt.  📊 Alleged Details: • ~54GB dataset • Price: ~$10,000 (negotiable) • Data offered via dark web channels  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Classic double extortion scenario: •Victim allegedly refused to pay ransom •Data now being sold publicly • Dataset size suggests: •Internal corporate data •Possible sensitive business or operational information  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of proprietary or pharmaceutical-related data • Regulatory and compliance impact • Follow-on attacks leveraging internal information • Reputational damage and potential legal consequences  📊 Status: Unverified — monitoring for validation and leak confirmation  💬 Failed ransom negotiations often lead to secondary monetization through data sales, increasing long-term impact beyond the initial breach.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #Ransomware #Healthcare #DailyDarkWeb

🇧🇷 Brazil Municipality Website Data Allegedly for Sale – Miracatu  A threat actor is advertising alleged access/data with the Municipality of Miracatu (São Paulo, Brazil), offering it for sale at a low price point.  📊 Alleged Details: • Target: miricatu.sp.gov.br • Sector: Government / Municipality • Price: $100 • Listing includes contact channels (Telegram / qTox)  🧠 About the Target: • Public service portal for residents and businesses • Handles: •Tax and billing services (IPTU, invoices) •Public transparency and budget data •Social services and enrollment systems •Jobs, tenders, and legal records  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Low pricing suggests: •Either limited dataset •Or quick monetization attempt • Municipal systems often expose: •Citizen data •Financial and administrative records • Possible source: •Web application vulnerability •Weak authentication or misconfiguration  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Exposure of citizen and taxpayer data • Fraud using municipal service context • Abuse of official communication channels  📊 Status: Unverified — monitoring for validation  💬 Local government platforms remain frequent targets due to broad citizen data access and often limited security maturity.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #Government #Brazil #DailyDarkWeb

🇵🇾 Paraguay Government Database Allegedly for Sale – ANDE (ande.gov.py)  A threat actor is advertising a database allegedly with ANDE (Administración Nacional de Electricidad), Paraguay’s national electricity provider.  📊 Alleged Details: • ~50,000 records • Format: CSV / SQL • Includes sample data and structured fields  📂 Allegedly Exposed Data Includes: • Full names • Email addresses • Phone numbers • Physical addresses • Customer messages / service requests  🧠 Threat Intelligence Insight: • Dataset structure suggests customer service / ticketing system exposure • Likely source: •Web application/database misconfiguration •Backend system compromise • Presence of user-submitted messages increases risk of context-rich social engineering  ⚠️ Potential Risks: • Targeted phishing using utility service context • Fraud leveraging billing or service request information • Exposure of citizen data linked to critical infrastructure  📊 Status: Unverified — data authenticity pending validation  💬 Breaches involving national utility providers can have broader implications due to their connection to critical infrastructure and citizen data.  #CyberSecurity #DataLeak #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #CriticalInfrastructure #Paraguay #DailyDarkWeb

This is one of my fave current strats by malware delivery systems:  They are not targeting end victims first. They’re targeting the trust layer.  Attackers compromise legitimate advertisers or small ad agencies. There are thousands of these entities, typically small teams (1–10 people) with weak IAM, poor MFA enforcement, and minimal audit visibility.  Once inside, they gain control of accounts connected to Google Ads or other trusted advertising pipelines. That access is the asset.  They then monetize it in two ways:  Direct use: run malicious ad campaigns (malware, phishing, fake installers) under a legitimate, reputation-backed account. Access brokering: resell campaign access to other operators who want distribution without building trust themselves.  Persistence is maintained deliberately:  Malicious ads are embedded within existing campaigns to avoid obvious anomalies.  Additional users are provisioned to maintain redundancy and prevent lockout.  UI-layer manipulation is used to reduce visibility (buried campaigns, naming collisions, low-visibility configurations).  Dwell time can extend for months because detection relies on owner-side observation, not platform enforcement.  In many cases, attackers inject their own funding into the account. This stabilizes spend patterns and avoids triggering billing or budget anomalies that would alert the legitimate owner. The objective is to preserve the integrity of the trust relationship for as long as possible.  The entire model hinges on one factor: once an account is trusted by the ad platform, that trust can be operationalized and resold.  ☯️

This is what a computer assembly plant in 1970s Silicon Valley looked like. San Jose, 1978.  Back when America actually built its own computers, IBM was doing it right here in San Jose. That campus is now a Lowe’s Home Improvement.  Manufacturing operations continued there into the 1990s, including the assembly of large disk drives in clean-room facilities. Over time, IBM gradually moved manufacturing overseas, primarily to countries in Asia such as China, Malaysia, and Thailand. Key manufacturing buildings on the Cottle Road campus were closed by 1996.  A few years later, IBM shifted its focus from manufacturing to research and development, and its current San Jose-area presence consists primarily of research labs rather than production facilities.  Today, the former Cottle Road location is occupied by the Lowe’s Home Improvement store at 5550 Cottle Rd, San Jose.  Look it up on Google Maps. The only remaining reminder is the IBM Cottle Road Campus Historic Landmark, which is visible in the Lowe’s parking lot.  source footage 🎥: KPIX
